author: phase-4-coordinator
content:
Implemented per-axis reductions in ferray-ma. New methods on MaskedArray:
- sum_axis(axis), mean_axis(axis), min_axis(axis), max_axis(axis), var_axis(axis), std_axis(axis) — return MaskedArray<T, IxDyn> with the reduced axis dropped
- count_axis(axis) — returns Array<u64, IxDyn> (not masked, since count is always defined)

All-masked lanes produce a masked output position holding fill_value. min_axis/max_axis are NaN-propagating per NumPy. Existing scalar methods (sum, mean, etc.) stay unchanged.

Internal helper reduce_axis<T, D, F>: walks the lanes via row-major flat-index computation, calls the kernel with a (T, bool) slice for each lane, and masks the output position when the kernel returns None (i.e., all-masked lane).

8 new tests covering: shape contraction, masked-element skipping, all-masked-lane masking, count, axis bounds errors, var/std consistency.

Closes #500.
